---
title: "Research"
---

My doctoral research focuses on probability and the mathematical analysis of Markov chain Monte Carlo algorithms.

## Hamiltonian Monte Carlo and Sampling Algorithms

My current research studies Hamiltonian Monte Carlo-type algorithms, coupling methods, and convergence behavior for probability distributions with geometric structure.

I am particularly interested in how algorithmic design choices affect mixing, stability, contraction, and convergence rates.

## Selected Research Themes

- Hamiltonian Monte Carlo  
- Coupling and path coupling  
- Mixing times  
- Wasserstein contraction  
- Sampling on manifolds  
- Strongly log-concave targets  
- Non-standard kinetic energies  
- Quantitative convergence bounds  

## Research-to-Industry Connection

Although my research is theoretical, the ideas connect naturally to computational statistics, Bayesian inference, simulation, uncertainty quantification, and scalable probabilistic modeling.
